_meta.description: Skill list and mechanics metadata for Mutants & Masterminds 4e character creation. All 'description' fields are original text written for this project (tooltip copy), not taken from the rulebook. Mechanical fields (ability, trainedOnly, action, focused, specializations) reflect game rules/facts, not copyrighted prose.

abilityKey.ATK: Attack (combat ability, not a core ability)

actionKey.simple_or_free: Simple or free action depending on use
actionKey.extended: Takes longer than a standard action; typically resolved outside of round-by-round action time

_meta.costRule: Normal skill ranks cost 1 Power Point per 2 ranks. Skills bought as a narrow focus (see 'focused': true) instead cost 1 Power Point per 4 ranks in that focus, but each focus is tracked as its own independent rank pool.
_meta.focusedNote: When 'focused' is true, a character must pick one or more specific focuses (e.g. a language, a field of expertise, a weapon type) and buy ranks separately per focus, effectively treating each focus as its own skill. When 'focused' is false, any 'specializations' listed are just common illustrative uses of the single skill, not separate rank pools.

skills.name: Acrobatics
skills.description: Flips, rolls, and fancy footwork — keeping your balance and grace in situations where an ordinary person would stumble.

skills.name: Athletics
skills.description: Raw physical exertion: scaling a wall, clearing a gap, outrunning trouble, or powering through open water.

skills.name: Close Combat
skills.description: Precision in melee range with a specific fighting style or weapon type — every focus you pick is trained and ranked on its own.

skills.name: Deception
skills.description: Selling a lie, a distraction, or a hidden agenda convincingly enough that people believe it.

skills.name: Disguise
skills.description: Building a convincing false identity out of makeup, costuming, and careful attention to detail.

skills.name: Expertise
focuses: Chosen freely per field of study, e.g. Science, Law, History, Streetwise
skills.description: Deep, formal knowledge in a specific academic, professional, or scholarly field — each field you study is its own focus. Because a focus is mandatory, Expertise ranks cost 1 PP per 4 ranks (the specialized rate).

skills.name: Insight
skills.description: Reading a person's true intentions, sincerity, or emotional state beneath what they're actually saying.

skills.name: Intimidation
skills.description: Making someone back down, comply, or lose their nerve through threats, presence, or sheer menace.

skills.name: Investigation
skills.description: Methodically working a case: searching a scene, chasing down leads, and drawing conclusions from the evidence.

skills.name: Languages
focuses: Chosen freely per language beyond the character's native tongue
skills.description: Fluent, literate command of a language other than your own — every additional language is its own focus.

skills.name: Magic
skills.description: Formal training in the practical craft of magic — casting, maintaining, and understanding arcane workings.

skills.name: Perception
specializations: By specific sense, e.g. sight, hearing, smell
skills.description: Staying alert to your surroundings — catching a sound, a shape in the shadows, or a detail everyone else missed.

skills.name: Performance
skills.description: Commanding an audience through a specific performing art — each discipline you train in is its own focus.

skills.name: Persuasion
skills.description: Winning people over honestly — through argument, charisma, or an appeal to their better nature.

skills.name: Ranged Combat
skills.description: Accuracy with a specific ranged weapon or attack type at a distance — every focus you pick is trained and ranked on its own.

skills.name: Sleight of Hand
skills.description: Quick, discreet handwork — palming an object, lifting a wallet, or planting something without being noticed.

skills.name: Stealth
skills.description: Staying unseen and unheard — sneaking past guards, hiding in plain sight, or trailing someone unnoticed.

skills.name: Survival
skills.description: Getting by in the wild — finding food, shelter, and a safe path through hostile terrain.

skills.name: Technology
skills.description: Working with machines and devices — building, fixing, hacking, or bypassing technology.

skills.name: Treatment
skills.description: Medical know-how — diagnosing injuries, providing first aid, and stabilizing someone in danger.

skills.name: Vehicles
skills.description: Confident, skillful handling of a vehicle, especially when conditions get difficult.
